entry.title: Washington Post: 27 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Violence against women rampant under Taliban, new report finds
  • Hungary’s Viktor Orban faces outrage after saying Europeans shouldn’t become ‘mixed race’
  • Emu and chef help stop driver fleeing crash site
  • Ukraine Live Briefing: U.S. made ‘substantial proposal’ for release of Griner and Whelan
  • Pentagon calls out China’s military threats as Taiwan tensions worsen
  • A Congo teen alleged rape by a priest. She had to flee. He can still say Mass.
  • Residential schools banned native languages. The Cree want theirs back.
  • Brittney Griner testifies at her trial in Russia, describes ordeal
  • France’s mustard shortage fuels drama and panic in grocery stores
  • 7.3-magnitude earthquake hits Philippines
  • How deforestation is pushing the Amazon toward a tipping point
  • Australian rugby league players boycott game over Pride-themed jerseys
  • Filipino lawmaker wants to punish people for ghosting
  • As they wait for weapons, Ukrainians hold the line with Soviet artillery
  • Taiwan hones invasion response amid China’s threats over Pelosi trip
  • 1,850-year-old Roman coin featuring zodiac sign found off Israeli coast
  • Stop the ‘steal’? Brazil’s Bolsonaro looks poised to start it.
  • David Trimble, peacemaker in Northern Ireland, dies at 77
  • Ukraine Live Briefing: Russia again targets Black Sea region amid grain deal fears; E.U. plans to ration gas
  • Israeli army says Hamas is rebuilding capabilities in Gaza
  • James Lovelock, creator of Gaia ecology theory, dies at 103
  • Followers of cleric enter Iraqi parliament in show of force
  • Pakistan uses boats, helicopters to evacuate flood victims
  • Pope in Quebec amid decline of Catholic Church in province
  • Ex-officers sentenced for violating George Floyd’s civil rights
  • A record number of trans and nonbinary people are running for office
  • Man charged with arson at dealership he said sold him busted car in ’86
  • Locals help police capture Oregon arson suspect by tying him to tree
  • A body found in 1948 became an Australian obsession. Now, there’s an ID.
  • Retired barber in Minn. cuts hair for donations — to feed the poor in South Africa
  • 19-year-old turns Gaetz insult into $115K abortion rights fundraiser
  • ‘Black Panther’ sequel: A rebuke of misogyny and racism in 2 minutes
  • Post Politics Now: Biden says he’s ‘feeling great’ after testing negative for the coronavirus
  • Co-defendant in Jan. 6 Sicknick assault case pleads guilty
  • The Georgia criminal investigation into Trump and his allies, explained
  • Post Politics Now: Trump returns to Washington; Biden on cusp of some wins
  • Justice Dept. investigating Trump’s actions in Jan. 6 criminal probe
  • D.C. man is 2nd to receive longest sentence in Jan. 6 police assault
  • Biden poised for big wins in Congress
  • Live updates: Fed hikes rates by three-quarters of a percentage point to fight inflation
  • Berkshire Hathaway-owned lender settles redlining case for $24 million
  • The fight to save energy by controlling your thermostat (and pool pump)
  • Recession experts don’t think we are in one and neither do I
  • As a cancer patient, ‘Cyberpunk 2077’ frees me from my mental prison
  • Federal Trade Commission sues to block Meta’s acquisition of VR company
  • Senate panel advances bills to boost children’s safety online
  • How to keep your phone working while traveling abroad
  • Residents find plenty of reasons to stay in Kings Park
  • Natural disasters are intensifying. Is your insurance policy sufficient?
  • The Midterm Guessing Game Just Keeps Getting Weirder
  • Low-Income Consumers Get a Break, But It Might Be Brief
  • Real Change at Credit Suisse? Don’t Hold Your Breath
  • Alphabet Investors Shouldn’t Ignore the Warning Signs
  • Why Another ‘Crypto Winter’ Is Test for Digital Money
  • Why Blackouts Are Still Crippling South Africa
  • Understanding the Unraveling of Tunisia’s Revolution
  • Understanding Monkeypox and How Outbreaks Spread
  • Can the West muster the will to face down Russia?
  • ‘Nope’ is one of the weirdest films this year. It just might inspire you.
  • Now we see the wisdom of the high court’s ‘vulgar cheerleader’ ruling
  • The U.S. is a lot stronger than Russia. We should act like it.
  • Is the U.S. economy about to slip on a banana?
  • The Fauci ‘ouchie’ was a near miracle. We need another.
  • When it rains on Donald Trump, it pours. And the deluge is just beginning.
  • A gun industry insider’s powerful testimony is cause for despair
  • In the rural West, ‘self-reliance’ can take a heavy toll on mental health
  • Here’s what’s offensive about Brad Pitt’s skirt
  • Cartoon by Clay Jones
  • The U.S. must end Israel’s impunity and investigate my aunt’s killing
  • Holding presidents accountable for their crimes is what democracies do
  • Rob Wittman’s reelection strategy
  • Why I’m protesting the Congressional Baseball Game
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Need a dose of humor in your day? Chat with Alexandra Petri.
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: Sydney Morning Herald: 27 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: Sydney Morning Herald: 27 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-27 19:30PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • ‘Whatever it takes, Bubbi’: Why you shouldn’t refuse TV’s The Offer
  • What does a person have to do to get their name in the dictionary?
  • Andrew Johns: Manly can’t win – and there will be unrest
  • ‘The shows won’t fit on the stage’: Why Melbourne could see a drought of opera and ballet
  • Gay A-League star slams Sea Eagles’ jersey boycott
  • Largest pink diamond in 300 years discovered in Angola
  • Meet the 13-year-old maths whiz who’s this year’s youngest HSC student
  • The blocker v the whisperer: Prizefight for preferences before state poll
  • NSW to ‘chase and recruit’ public school teachers as private sector snaps up graduates
  • State Liberal candidate was deemed ‘too extreme’ for Scott Morrison’s campaign
  • What the Greens decide now on climate will affect left politics long term
  • Affordable housing, animal shelter lose out in North Melbourne’s Arden precinct plan
  • Council’s trucks have mixed recycling with rubbish for ‘more than a decade’
  • Majority of animals caught in shark nets last year were threatened or protected species
  • Climate bill to make government fossil fuel investment harder
  • Mystery surrounds lives and deaths of Saudi sisters in ‘unfriendly’ units
  • Pauline Hanson shows Clive Palmer’s man around the Canberra bubble
  • Magnitude-7.1 earthquake hits Philippines’ most populous island
  • WHO chief advises reducing sex partners to avoid monkeypox
  • Aged care providers can seek exemption to Labor’s 24/7 nurse mandate
  • The latest illustrations from artist Cathy Wilcox
  • Cartoons of the week
  • The latest illustrations from artist Andrew Dyson
  • The latest illustrations from artist Matt Golding
  • Best of cartoons, July 29, 2022
  • Best of cartoons, July 27, 2022

  • OECD head Mathias Cormann dodges questions about WA Liberals ‘mess left behind’
  • Indigenous Voice could drive a wedge between Australians: Price
  • Where inflation is hurting household budgets the most
  • Construction watchdog vows to keep prosecuting unions after ‘negligible’ changes
  • The slugger, the jabber and the ref: the new order in question time
  • ‘If you cannot see it, you cannot be it’: Why representation matters to new Greens MP
  • China wants to ‘bully and dominate’ the Indo-Pacific: top US general
  • Chalmers faces his bleak Thursday but it’s no Black Tuesday
  • Great demand but less fortune for Morrison on Pentecostal speakers circuit
  • Rainbow jersey refusal is homophobia, plain and simple
  • Can you answer these 50 HSC questions?
  • HSC students display resilience and strength
  • How to set yourself up for HSC success
  • How to cope with the stresses of the HSC
  • Winning the numbers game in HSC maths
  • The science student who took on the world - and won
  • History, business and legal studies: top tips from a HSIE teacher
  • Finding the perfect pitch in music, art and drama
  • A sporting chance through perseverance
  • VET pathway led to electrician career for Abram
  • The secret to getting top marks in HSC English
  • In the Herald: July 28, 1942
  • From the Archives, 1990: Abortion protest leads to scuffles
  • Sydney mountaineer dies climbing world’s second-highest peak
  • Giving new meaning to a birdie blitz
  • Wuhan locks down 1 million residents in echo of pandemic start
  • Dozens killed and injured in ‘social media-fuelled’ anti-UN protests
  • Gallery: The best photos from around the world
  • US Justice Department probing Trump’s efforts to overturn election
  • Russia to drop out of International Space Station after 2024
  • ‘Oh my god’: Presenter faints, ending Tory leadership debate
  • Birmingham Games set to challenge British colonialism, celebrate diversity
  • China to raise concerns over AUKUS submarine deal at UN treaty meeting
  • Top bankers, business leaders say Brazil’s democracy in ‘grave danger’
  • ‘Risk is clear’: China seeks to infiltrate the US Federal Reserve, senior Senate Republican says
  • Education as important as Voice to Parliament, says Aboriginal leader
  • Record prices in Brisbane property market as other cities slow down
  • Federal Reserve unleashes another big rate hike in bid to curb inflation
  • Westpac, NAB leave door open on oil and gas finance amid climate push
  • Port backflip a boost for Viva’s Victorian gas terminal
  • Rio Tinto halves dividend as falling iron ore price hits profit
  • ‘Unacceptable’: NBN Co sent back to square one on pricing model
  • Instant gratification: The lure of a 10-minute mortgage approval
  • ‘Critical’: Westpac unveils new targets to cut carbon emissions
  • ‘Ansett old boys’ club’: Virgin pilot details Hrdlicka bullying claims
  • Pulling the strings: Putin is gaming Europe’s energy crisis
  • The Wrap: ASX undaunted by 20-year high inflation figures
  • Credit Suisse CEO set to exit as upheaval continues at scandal-plagued giant
  • World’s richest family loses $16 billion in one day in Walmart rout
  • Mark Zuckerberg’s biggest bet is not paying off
  • ‘A real winner’: Paper Girls will fill the Stranger Things-size hole in your life
  • A true chippie off the old Block: Scott Cam builds his own house
  • Morals and beliefs are on the line in uncompromising prison drama Time
  • Bob Marley to U2: Music trailblazer tells all in celebrity-strewn memoir
  • Dan Hong searches for the world’s greatest street foods
  • What it was like on Neighbours if you weren’t a star
  • Hitchcock and a train strike make for a superb French drama
  • By the end, I was long past caring about this film’s mirthless chuckles
  • Your ABC Exposed doco lands some punches but exposes little more than an agenda
  • Gorillaz stage a feel-good pop party with feel-bad undercurrents
  • Knives Out lookalike puts a French twist on the murder mystery
  • Is the future of Australian TV in jeopardy?
  • ‘I hope you took acid’ – Strokes deliver weird, robotic post-Splendour show
  • Not many shows can stun crowds into silence. This one did
  • You’d need a heart of stone not to be moved by Couples Therapy Australia
  • Shopify culls Australian staff in late night raid
  • Instagram to Jenner: We’re not turning back on ‘TikTok’ changes
Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

entry.title: NY Times: 27 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • What Fed Rate Increases Mean for Mortgages, Credit Cards and More

entry.title: Financial Times: 27 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Fed raises rates by 0.75 points for second month in a row
  • A Fed-induced recession is a medicine worse than the disease
  • Wall Street rallies after Fed chair suggests rate rises may slow
  • How bad will the global food crisis get?
  • US and China are entering a trap of their own making
  • Memo to Wall Street: don’t touch that delete button!
  • Top US regulator fires warning shot after Apple’s push into lending
  • European gas price rise accelerates as Russia cuts flows
  • Oil and gas majors: time for a transformative clean energy deal?

  • Ukrainian strikes hit key supply route in Russian-controlled Kherson region

  • Reckitt gains from rising medicine sales as Covid-19 beds in

  • Johnson to increase House of Lords numbers despite downsizing pledge

  • Mining outlook darkens as twin threats loom
  • Australia’s ‘avo-lanche’ forces farmers to look to Asia and UK for sales
  • El Salvador seeks to allay default fears with sovereign bond buyback

  • Turkey’s new power plant exposes ‘huge contradictions’ of net zero pledge
  • Lloyds: clean banking proposition shows benefits of rising rates
  • Haleon/Reckitt Benckiser: Covid offers cold cure makers a long-lasting boost

  • I want to become a TV presenter but can’t see a way into the industry
  • Is my trust affected by new HMRC rules?
  • The UK won’t take fraud seriously until we take the SFO seriously

entry.title: Guardian: 27 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • US Federal Reserve announces three-quarter-point interest rate increase to cool inflation – live
  • South Africa chase 235 to beat England: first T20 international – live!
  • Germany v France: Women’s Euro 2022 semi-final – live!
  • San Francisco couple ticketed after curb painted red – while their car was parked
  • Greece rolls out red carpet for crown prince, as Khashoggi killing falls off agenda
  • Fed announces another three-quarter-point increase in interest rates
  • ‘Imprudent’ priest uses inflatable mattress as altar during mass in sea
  • UK scientists take ‘promising’ step towards single Covid and cold vaccine

  • Germany accuses Russia of ‘power play’ as gas pipeline supply drops by half

  • Ashley Judd says she met man who raped her, as part of ‘restorative justice’ process
  • US military makes plans for Nancy Pelosi’s potential Taiwan visit
  • ‘I feel smug going in the short queue’: UK readers on getting EU passports
  • Manchester United complete Lisandro Martínez signing for initial £48.3m
  • Does the Covid vaccine really affect your period? Here’s what our study found | Viki Male
  • Lionesses roar into Wembley final – Women’s Football Weekly
  • Alessia Russo banished all fear with the cheeky backheel that foxed Sweden | Jonathan Liew
  • The faded elegance of Odesa, gateway to Ukraine – archive, 1960
  • Premier League 2022-23 preview No 6: Chelsea
  • ‘Neighbours pulled me through the bleakest points in my life’: readers say farewell to the Australian soap
  • The Euros prove it: women's football is not like men's – and that's good | Jen Offord
  • Ukraine steps up attacks on Russian-occupied Kherson
  • Hit the Road review – irrepressible defiance in beautifully composed debut feature
  • ‘A dangerous moment’: China warns of consequences if Pelosi visits Taiwan
  • Why is everyone saying Instagram is rubbish now – and what’s TikTok got to do with it?
  • Mystery of Somerton man’s identity solved after 73 years, researchers in Australia say
  • Air pollution is ‘likely’ to raise dementia risk, find UK government experts
  • Springsteen tickets are going for a whopping $4,000 – what else are we paying dynamic prices for?
  • A moment that changed me: I was in a chaotic place after an MS diagnosis and escaped into woodcarving
  • Seals and shipwrecks: lapping up the beauty of the northern Aegean
  • Foot-and-mouth disease: how is Indonesia trying to control the outbreak?
  • ‘I’d rather be a tramp than reform my old bands’: Lawrence on life as British music’s greatest also-ran
  • ‘I was not being loved. I was just a body’: Mena Suvari on surviving sexual abuse, acting and American Beauty
  • The Choco Taco, a beloved American treat, is dead. Or is it?
  • ‘This result will go around the world,’ says Sarina Wiegman after England win
Latest international news, sport and comment from the Guardian

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 27 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 27 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-27 18:55PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Classic Hong Kong bakery Happy Cake Shop to close after 45 years in business as landlord takes back premises
  • UN human rights watchdog urges Hong Kong to take ‘concrete steps’ to repeal national security law; government slams ‘unfair’ criticism
  • Hong Kong power firm CLP to offer HK$100 vouchers to blackout-hit residents, after cable fire causes worst outage in decades
  • Hong Kong property tycoon accuses US of intending to destroy city’s financial status and mainland China
  • Hong Kong butcher may face HK$50,000 fine and jail time after banned chemical found on beef sample
  • Activists plan to vote for ‘Hong Kong parliament’ abroad amid questions about its legitimacy
  • Return of mainland Chinese visitors, launch of ‘insurance connect’ a ‘huge opportunity’ for Hong Kong insurers
  • Hong Kong publisher launches first English volume in chronicle of city’s 7,000-year history
  • ‘Funny prank’ on former Hong Kong leader: ex-aviation worker pleads guilty to criminal damage for changing name of Leung Chun-ying’s son in flight booking and ordering extra services
  • Hong Kong’s anti-graft agency charges 2 men for allegedly urging others to spoil ballots in Legco election
  • Hong Kong police arrest 18 in crackdown on fraud syndicate for sending bogus applications to qualify for jobless subsidy scheme
  • Hong Kong poised to leap ahead, in ‘enviable’ spot as East-meets-West hub, John Lee says as he hails city’s talent and resilience
  • Raising Hong Kong’s plastic bag levy from proposed HK$1 to HK$2 will further reduce usage by 40 per cent, lawmaker says
  • Hong Kong’s lived-in home prices slump to lowest level since December 2020 as caution prevails ahead of rate hike
  • Hong Kong health experts review prescription rules for Covid drugs at public hospitals in a bid to combat rise in virus-related deaths
  • Hong Kong to launch HK$5 billion tech fund in August, finance chief reveals
  • Call for safety measures after member of popular Hong Kong boy band Mirror falls off stage
  • Hong Kong must raise interest rates but wild swing in property prices unlikely, finance chief says
  • To chart a new economic course for Hong Kong, build a new north-south rail line
  • US Senate passes bill providing US$52 billion in subsidies to semiconductor industry
  • China ‘confident’ on 2030 climate goal as summer heatwaves worsen global energy crisis
  • Xi Jinping tells cadres to prepare for ‘critical moment’ for China ahead of Communist Party congress
  • Canadian cancer scientist hoping for role on China’s Tiangong Space Station
  • Huawei launches updated operating system HarmonyOS 3, new ride-hailing platform
  • Call between Xi and Biden won’t break China-US deadlock, observers say
  • China launches new satellite in ‘important step’ towards global quantum communications network
  • US aircraft carrier group heads towards Taiwan as tension over Nancy Pelosi’s possible visit continues to grow
  • Will Chinese President Xi Jinping be given the formal title of ‘the people’s leader’?
  • Chinese EV maker Li Auto’s L9 SUV runs off road, hits barrier during test drive, could derail sales after news goes viral
  • Coronavirus: China’s travel sector sees ‘revenge’ rebound, but outbreaks and lockdowns loom large
  • Chinese, Indonesian first ladies watch a folk music performance in Beijing
  • The US military is making plans in the Indo-Pacific in case Nancy Pelosi travels to Taiwan
  • Coronavirus: Shenzhen controls outbreak while Wuhan district imposes lockdown
  • Should we worry about space debris from Chinese rockets hitting Earth?
  • Croatia opens China-built bridge that bypasses Bosnia to get to Dubrovnik – a boon for tourists, businesses and residents
  • Beijing says it seeks peaceful reunification with Taiwan, but warns island over separatist forces
  • China’s industrial profits rise for first time since March as coronavirus curbs ease, but concerns remain
  • How Nasa’s James Webb and China’s Xuntian space telescopes could cooperate in future
  • China orders Covid-19 community helpers to follow local leadership
  • Beijing to shake up housing market near elite schools with new education policy
  • New Covid-19 origins studies point to Wuhan market, not Chinese lab leak
  • Beyond China: US’ Xinjiang cotton ban has far-reaching implications, even for Asian alternatives
  • How instability in Afghanistan raises the global risk of narco-terrorism
  • South Korea’s risky ‘Kill Chain’ plans to counter North Korea nuclear threat may raise eyebrows in China
  • Japan’s netizens express glee, relief over execution of Akihabara mass murderer
  • Japan, Indonesia agree to boost naval security ties, with an eye on China
  • Singapore extends visit permit for Sri Lanka’s self-exiled former president Gotabaya Rajapaksa
  • Thailand cashes in on cannabis legalisation with surge in weed-infused products like milk tea and toothpaste
  • South Korea’s Yoon Suk-yeol takes aim at police as he battles record low approval ratings
  • Powerful 7-magnitude earthquake strikes the Philippines, killing at least 5
  • Indian actor Ranveer Singh’s nude photo shoot attracts police complaints: ‘This is vulgar. We can see his bum’
  • US officials call China’s actions in the South China Sea unsafe and increasing
  • US Federal Reserve unleashes another big rate hike in bid to curb inflation
  • US gun makers made over US$1 billion from AR-15 sales in past decade
  • Joe Biden ends Covid-19 isolation after testing negative twice
  • Critics voice concerns after Scotland puts Ukrainian refugees on cruise ship
  • Iran hanged 251 people at a ‘horrifying pace’ in first half of 2022
  • Scientists warn window to stop monkeypox spread closing fast
  • Rare pink diamond found in Angola may be the largest seen in 300 years
  • Russia claims capture of Ukraine’s Vuhlehirska power station, as UK reports ‘tactical advances’ by Wagner mercenaries
  • Russia doing better than expected despite sanctions over war in Ukraine, IMF says
  • Russia’s decision to drop out of International Space Station catches Nasa off guard. What now?
  • New pictures show Saudi plan for futuristic mirrored skyscraper megacity NEOM
  • Indian peacekeepers among 15 killed in anti-UN protests in Congo’s east
  • Ukraine war: An inside look at prison life for captured Russian troops
  • ‘Orwellian’ facial recognition surveillance cameras in UK’s Southern Co-operative stores challenged by rights group
  • Taliban crackdown on Afghan women escalates while world looks away, report finds
  • China tariff decision unlikely before call between Joe Biden and Xi Jinping
  • Donald Trump returns to Washington, hints at third presidential run
  • Meet Freya, the 600kg walrus sinking boats and capturing hearts in Europe
  • South Korean chip maker SK Hynix reports record revenue despite ‘difficult’ business environment
  • Country Garden, China’s top property developer, plans US$360 million share sale to refinance debt
  • Miniso loses US$281 million in stock market rout as short-seller Blue Orca attacks business model, insider ties
  • A developing-country debt crisis is looming, but it can be headed off
  • Binance’s founder CZ sues Modern Media for libel as the richest man in the cryptocurrency world steps up action to defend his reputation
  • Alibaba, HSBC pressure Hong Kong stocks before Fed decision while Country Garden sinks on stock placement
  • Thailand’s plan to allow foreigners to own land may worsen oversupply of flats in Bangkok
  • Shopify will cut 10 per cent of its staff after Covid-19 expansion bet fails to pay off
  • Disney is preparing for the metaverse, but what is it, and what can video game platforms Roblox and Fortnite tell us about its potential impact?
  • ‘So much Chinese culture came from the outside’: nomads, the Silk Road and developments in ancient art in northern China
  • Septet: The Story of Hong Kong movie review – Johnnie To-produced feature sees seven directors put their feelings for the city on film
  • What is blokecore? Soccer shirts, Adidas sneakers – think British working men in the 1990s – with fans in Rihanna, A$AP Rocky
  • An island retreat with old Hong Kong vibes, rattan furniture and a rooftop for entertaining guests
  • ‘A sane person would have stopped’: Covid-19 news tweeter in Hong Kong, Tripperhead, on feeling burned out but compelled to carry on
  • Bruce Lee, Elvis and Jackie Chan at Madame Tussauds in Hong Kong – when the waxworks museum opened in 2000, and the figure who snubbed it
  • From Hello Kitty to Rolex watches, custom cakes by Hong Kong baker are so realistic you’ll do a double take
  • NewJeans vs Le Sserafim, NCT vs NCT: how K-pop labels manufacture in-house rivalry between groups to fatten their profits
  • The fabulous life (and wardrobe) of Cardi B’s daughter Kulture: from Dior and Chanel shopping sprees with mum, to Swarovski-encrusted Hermès Birkins, a Rolls-Royce car seat and her own Richard Mille
  • Inside Justin Bieber’s Hawaii holiday rental home, up for auction: the singer paid US$10,000 a night to stay at Waterfalling Estate, which also hosted the Love Island and Ex on the Beach cast
  • Is the Porsche 911 the world’s most timeless sports car? Almost 60 years on, it has the same iconic look while Ferraris and Lamborghinis are continually replaced with newer models
  • Are Zenith watches now as prized as Rolex and Patek Philippe? The LVMH-owned brand’s Chronomaster Sport and Defy Skyline models now have waiting lists as long as the world’s most coveted Swiss timepieces
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: ChannelNewsAsia: 27 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • WHO says more than 18,000 cases of monkeypox globally, most in Europe

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
